# P1 byte-parity golden for the daemon-spawned vhost-user-media (video)
# sidecar. Captures BOTH the argv `nixlingd` will exec AND the kernel-8
# wire-contract pins from `pkgs/spectrum-ch/cloud-hypervisor/0003-vhost-
# user-media-device.patch`. Generated by
# `nixling_host::video_argv::tests::audit_parity_snapshot_line`.
#
# Wire-contract field provenance (every value here is hard-coded in the
# CH patch above; this golden mirrors them so any CH-side drift surfaces
# as a single byte diff in CI):
#
#   virtio_id=48                       -> `const VIRTIO_ID_MEDIA: u32 = 48`
#   num_queues=2                       -> `const NUM_QUEUES: u16 = QUEUE_SIZES.len() as _`
#   queue_size=256                     -> `const QUEUE_SIZES: &[u16] = &[256, 256]`
#   shm_region_bytes=268435456         -> `VhostSharedMemoryRegion { length: 256 * 1024 * 1024 }`
#   vring_base=0                       -> `self.vu_common.vring_bases = Some(vec![0; queues.len()])`
#   protocol_flags=BACKEND_REQ|REPLY_ACK|SHMEM_MAP_CROSVM
#                                      -> `acked_protocol_features` mask in CH patch
#   mmio_allocator=pci-mem64           -> `self.pci_segments[..].mem64_allocator.allocate(...)`
#
# The argv line is the per-VM crosvm vhost-user-media backend invocation.
# The protocol-flag list is lexicographically sorted for golden stability.
/nix/store/CROSVMVIDEOCROSVMVIDEO-crosvm/bin/crosvm device video-decoder --socket-path /run/nixling-video/corp-desktop/video.sock --backend vaapi
virtio_id=48 num_queues=2 queue_size=256 shm_region_bytes=268435456 vring_base=0 protocol_flags=BACKEND_REQ|REPLY_ACK|SHMEM_MAP_CROSVM mmio_allocator=pci-mem64
